title: Non-record: trigram phrase-memory ablation on 1×H100: negative result (1.2791 BPB best)
body:
## What this is

A controlled ablation testing whether TrigramHash lookup tables improve a tuned 1×H100 backbone at the 16MB parameter-golf scale.

## Results

| Run | Params | Trigrams | val_bpb | Size |
|-----|--------|----------|---------|------|
| Full backbone control | 25.3M | None | **1.2791** | 21.6MB |
| Slim control | 20.6M | None | **1.3007** | 17.4MB |
| Slim + 2048×64 | ~19M | 2048×64 | **1.3029** | 17.3MB |
| Slim + 4096×96 | ~21M | 4096×96 | **1.3040** | 17.9MB |

Fair comparison (same slim backbone): no-trigram beats both trigram variants. This is consistent with PR #486's note that "trigram ablation confirmed negative at small scale" but provides a published controlled comparison with actual numbers.

## Why this might be useful

- Saves others from spending compute testing trigrams in isolation at this configuration
- Suggests the byte budget might be better spent on backbone capacity than static lookup tables
- Open question: does this reverse on 8×H100 with 13K+ training steps?

## Hardware

1×H100 SXM, full FineWeb SP1024 dataset, 600s wallclock. No artifact fit under 16MB unfortunately and I ran out of credits to try a few smaller runs.  This is an exploratory non-record submission.
